author: bharath.vedartham
content: I think it makes sense for the builder to only construct the envelope only if the proposer has broadcasted a beacon block with the bid included

author: bharath.vedartham
content: I ll update the API to just pass in the beacon block root instead of the full block. I feel like that should suffice for now

author: 0xunclebill
content: for envelope by root requests is it ok to serve an envelope without checking that its canonical? its a bit awkward that for a given block root, we need to figure out it child block root and then compare the childs bid against the payload before serving it.

author: 0xunclebill
content: for envelopes by range this is much less awkward to deal with since we are just iterating over a range of slots

author: 0xunclebill
content: also are details like this worth including in the spec? feels like it should since it affects how we'd penalize peers

author: potuz
content: Just like block by root you must serve them even if they aren't canonical

author: potuz
content: Otherwise we wouldn't be able to sync other branches

author: 0xunclebill
content:
yeah makes sense

btw how are you handling verifying the last requested slot in a envelope by range request? you fetch its child block i assume? and in the case where there are skipped slots you just iterate until you find its child block? that edge case is a bit akward for us too

author: potuz
content:
I haven't seen the RPC code in Prysm but for finalized data we should just simply serve it and trust that they are canonical (I know this isn't working cause I'm pretty sure we aren't pruning yet, so I think we check explicitly with child blocks). For unfinalized data forkchoice has this information for us since every node has a pointer to the best node, hence checking if something is canonical is O(1). 

But anyway serving unfinalized ranges should be trivial just give the best child until you find head or a higher slot than the requested slot

author: stefanbratanov
content: What is the pandaops page for devnet-0, want to test checkpoint syncing?

author: qu0b
content: <https://checkpoint-sync.epbs-devnet-0.ethpandaops.io/> or <https://beacon.epbs-devnet-0.ethpandaops.io/>

author: bharath.vedartham
content:
The spec doesn't mention how a proposer should select a bid from a list of received bids. i.e if i receive 10 bids via the gossip layer, how is the winning bid selected. I think ideally it would be the highest bid amongst them. 
Should this be mentioned in the spec or is it left out to give the proposer freedom to choose the bid however they desire?

author: bharath.vedartham
content:
I believe all the spec says is:
```
Select one bid and set block.body.signed_execution_payload_bid = signed_execution_payload_bid." 
```

author: stefanbratanov
content: i like this way and then clients can do whatever they prefer

author: bharath.vedartham
content:
In the p2p interface for `execution_payload_bid` , we have:
```
- _[IGNORE]_ this bid is the highest value bid seen for the corresponding slot
  and the given parent block hash.
```
1. I think this should also consider the parent block root too and not just the parent hash. 
2. I think this probably indirectly suggests that the proposer will always pick the highest value bid because only the highest value bid will be forwarded to it

author: potuz
content: I think this should not be specified

author: potuz
content: Fwiw <@358120958726373381> I'm implementing sync from scratch right now and this is what I'll do. We don't necessarily receive blocks and Payloads from the same peer when requesting by range. So I'll unconditionally downscore peers that do not return a self consistent list of payloads (by hash dependency). In addition if the peer is the same as the peer that sent us the blocks I'll downscore if the Payloads are not consistent with the bids

author: bharath.vedartham
content: Following this, i don't think we would want to spec how to handle selecting b/w locally built bids, p2p bids and bids from the builder-api and let clients do what they prefer?

content:

* https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/pull/4979
<@&1417820113981145228> please review when you have time.

author: jtraglia
content: Add PTC lookbehind

author: jih2nn
content:
After discussing with <@592004585506340885>, considering a tuple of `(parent_block_root, parent_block_hash)` seems right here. Considering only one of them could be undesirable when there are forks.

Regarding 2, I personally feel like I agree with you, especially when taking the existential purpose of trustless payment into account, but couldn't come up with a good solution on the spot.

author: jih2nn
content: Maybe it can be loosened up to, let's say, top 2 bids, but I don't have any strong opinion.

author: jtraglia
content: Yup agreed. Just made this PR: https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/pull/5001

author: jtraglia
content: This isn't my idea & I forget who mentioned it, but I would support a PR which added something to the proposer preferences for this. Which allows proposers to tell the network what is most important to them. This would only work if we can make a list of options & easily check them though.

author: jtraglia
content: Thanks for reviewing y'all. Just enabled auto-merge.
